MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
Off Calendar May-13-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances in Open court. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

2 - DEFENDANTs UPGRADE, INC.,and CROSS RIVER BANK's MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE.

Defendants to provide a proposed order for each motion.

Defendants Upgrade, Inc. and Cross River Bank's unopposed Motion to Admit Counsel Joseph A. Apatov Pro Hac Vice is GRANTED. Good cause appearing, Counsel Joseph A. Apatov may appear as counsel pro hac vice for moving party in this action.

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File Cross-Complaint; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Thomas S. Van; [Proposed] Order; [Proposed] Cross-Complaint
Off Calendar May-13-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances in Open Court. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

1 - DEFENDANTs UPGRADE, INC.,and CROSS RIVER BANK's MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File Cross-Complaint.

Defendants to provide a proposed order for each motion.

Defendants Upgrade, Inc. and Cross River Bank's unopposed Motion for Leave to File Cross-Complaint is GRANTED.

Leave to file a cross-complaint "may be granted in the interest of justice at any time during the course of the action." (Code of Civil Procedure section 428.50(c).) "A motion to file a cross-complaint at any time during the course of the action must be granted unless bad faith of the moving party is demonstrated where forfeiture would otherwise result. Factors such as oversight, inadvertence, neglect, mistake or other cause, are insufficient grounds to deny the motion unless accompanied by bad faith." (Silver Organizations Ltd. v. Frank (1990) 217 Cal.App.3d 94, 99.)

Good cause appearing, the court grants Defendants leave to file the proposed cross-complaint. Defendants must file his cross-complaint, substantially identical to Exhibit A to the Choo Declaration, within ten days of entry of this order.

Order is signed

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
